steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A line in slope-intercept form is $y = mx + b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept. We need slope $= -\frac{1}{4}$ and the line passes through $(0, 19)$, so $b = 19$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = -\frac{1}{4}x - 19$': Slope is correct but y-intercept is $-19$, not $19$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y = -\frac{1}{4}x + 19$': Slope $-\frac{1}{4}$ and y-intercept $19$ — both correct. ✓
  • Choice C — '$y = \frac{1}{4}x - 19$': Slope is positive and y-intercept is wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = \frac{1}{4}x + 19$': Slope is positive (wrong sign). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
